perturbation.original_text: The Corporation represents, warrants and covenants to the Agents, and acknowledges that the Agents are relying upon such representations, warranties and covenants, that: (a) the Corporation (i) is duly amalgamated under the Business Corporations Act (Ontario) (the "Act") and is up-to-date in respect of all material corporate filings and is in good standing under such Act; (ii) has all requisite corporate power, authority and capacity to carry on its business as now conducted and to own, lease and operate its properties and assets (including as described in the Public Disclosure); and (iii) has all requisite corporate power, authority and capacity to create, issue and sell the Offered Shares, to enter into this Agency Agreement and the Compensation Option Certificates, and to carry out the provisions contained in hereunder and thereunder;
perturbation.changed_text: The Corporation represents, warrants and covenants to the Agents, and acknowledges that the Agents are relying upon such representations, warranties and covenants, that: (a) the Corporation (i) may not be duly amalgamated under the Business Corporations Act (Ontario) (the "Act") and may not be up-to-date in respect of all material corporate filings and may not be in good standing under such Act; (ii) may not have all requisite corporate power, authority and capacity to carry on its business as now conducted and to own, lease and operate its properties and assets (including as described in the Public Disclosure); and (iii) may not have all requisite corporate power, authority and capacity to create, issue and sell the Offered Shares, to enter into this Agency Agreement and the Compensation Option Certificates, and to carry out the provisions contained in hereunder and thereunder;
perturbation.explanation: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating that the Corporation may not have the necessary corporate standing and power, directly conflicting with the original representation that it does. This creates uncertainty about the Corporation's legal authority to enter into the agreement, which is a critical element for the agents relying on these warranties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The sale of the Offered Shares shall be subject to the representations, warranties and covenants of the Corporation contained in this Agreement being accurate as of the date of this Agreement and as of the Closing Date, to the Corporation having performed all of its obligations under this Agreement and to the following additional conditions, and the Agents shall have the right on the Closing Date on behalf of Subscribers for Offered Shares to withdraw, all Subscription Agreements delivered and not previously withdrawn by Subscribers unless such conditions have been satisfied:
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces uncertainty about the Corporation's ability to fulfill its obligations by stating it "may not" possess the requisite corporate power and standing. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states the sale of shares is subject to the 'representations, warranties and covenants of the Corporation contained in this Agreement being accurate'. The 'may not' undermines the accuracy of those representations, creating a direct contradiction as the agents' right to withdraw subscriptions depends on the corporation's representations being accurate, which the change casts doubt upon.

perturbation.original_text: The Corporation agrees that the Agents are acting as agents of the Corporation in seeking purchasers of Offered Shares without underwriter liability, and nothing in this Agreement or any other agreement shall require the Agents to purchase any of the Offered Shares in connection with the Offering.
perturbation.changed_text: The Corporation agrees that the Agents are acting as agents of the Corporation in seeking purchasers of Offered Shares with underwriter liability, and this Agreement requires the Agents to purchase all of the Offered Shares in connection with the Offering.
perturbation.explanation: The modification directly contradicts the original statement by asserting that the Agents *do* have underwriter liability and *are* required to purchase all offered shares. This is a major reversal of the agreement's nature, creating uncertainty over the Agents' obligations. Specifically contradicted is section 8 by changing agency basis to one of guaranteed purchase.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Agency Basis The Corporation agrees that the Agents are acting as agents of the Corporation in seeking purchasers of Offered Shares without underwriter liability, and nothing in this Agreement or any other agreement shall require the Agents to purchase any of the Offered Shares in connection with the Offering.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text by asserting that the Agents *do* have underwriter liability and *are* required to purchase all offered shares, which is a complete reversal of the original agreement's agency basis. Specifically, changing 'without underwriter liability' to 'with underwriter liability' and removing 'nothing in this Agreement or any other agreement shall require the Agents to purchase any of the Offered Shares in connection with the Offering' to 'this Agreement requires the Agents to purchase all of the Offered Shares in connection with the Offering' creates a direct conflict. The contradicted text, explicitly stating that the Agents are acting as agents 'without underwriter liability', is completely negated by the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: Time is of the essence in the performance of the parties' respective obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Time is NOT of the essence in the performance of the parties' respective obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This edit directly contradicts the clause stating time is of the essence. This introduces confusion about the importance of deadlines and timely performance, impacting all sections where deadlines are specified and making enforcement uncertain. Changing this, has a direct impact on section 6, because of the date that is specified in the closing date.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Closing The sale of the Offered Shares shall be completed at the Closing Time at the offices of Corporation's Counsel in Toronto, Ontario or at such other place as the Corporation and the Agents may agree. At the Closing Time, the Corporation shall deliver to the Agents: (a) the opinions, certificates and agreements referred to in Section 9 and all other documents required to be provided by the Corporation to the Agents pursuant to this Agreement and the Subscription Agreements; (b) except for any Offered Shares offered or sold in the United States which shall be represented by individual definitive share certificates, one or more definitive share certificate(s)/and or book-entry only securities, duly registered as the Lead Agent may direct the Corporation; (c) the Corporation's receipt for payment by the Agents of an amount equal to the aggregate purchase price for the Offered Shares sold pursuant to the Offering; and (d) such further documentation as may be contemplated by this Agreement or as Agents' Counsel or the applicable regulatory authorities may reasonably require; against delivery by the Agents of: 16 (e) all duly completed Subscription Agreements tendered by the Subscribers for the Offered Shares being issued and sold and, where applicable, all completed forms, schedules and certificates contemplated by such Subscription Agreements; (f) a wire transfer of immediately available funds in an amount equal to the aggregate purchase price for the Offered Shares sold pursuant to the Offering, less an amount equal to the Agents' Fee and the costs and expenses of the Agent provided for in Section 14; and (g) the Agents' receipt for the Agents' Fee and the definitive certificates delivered to the Agents in accordance with Section 6(b).
perturbation.justification: The original text stated "Time is of the essence...", which legally implies that deadlines and timely performance are critical for fulfilling the contract obligations. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating "Time is NOT of the essence...". This creates a clear conflict because Section 6 specifies a "Closing Time" and requires actions to be completed at that time. If time is not of the essence, the enforceability and significance of the Closing Time become ambiguous, potentially leading to disputes over whether the obligations in Section 6 must be strictly adhered to. This aligns with the definition of "Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction" as the change introduces conflicting obligations concerning deadlines.
